channel.title: Headlines - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

1. Alexander Zverev wins the French Open to finally earn a 1st Grand Slam title  NPR

  4. Nadal, Alcaraz congratulate Zverev on Roland Garros triumph  ATP Tour
  5. Alexander Zverev wins first grand slam after holding off Cobolli in French Open  The Guardian

item.title: Nearly half of Americans say they're worse off financially than a year ago, NY Fed finds - CBS News
item.description:
1. Nearly half of Americans say they're worse off financially than a year ago, NY Fed finds  CBS News
  2. Americans ‘don’t like the look of things’ and are growing more worried about their job and their finances  CNN
  3. Household worries over finances hit highest level since July 2022, New York Fed survey shows  CNBC
  4. New York Fed survey: Share of households reporting a worse financial situation hits highest level since 2023  Yahoo Finance
  5. Prospects for Job Seekers Seen Worsening, NY Fed Survey Says  Bloomberg.com

item.title: Broncos' Cooper pleads not guilty; trial set for late July - ESPN
item.description:
1. Broncos' Cooper pleads not guilty; trial set for late July  ESPN

item.title: NVIDIA and SK hynix Announce Multiyear Technology Partnership to Advance Memory for AI Factories - NVIDIA Newsroom
item.description:

item.title: Why Are Birthrates Down? Two New Studies Point to Phones. - The New York Times
item.description:
1. Why Are Birthrates Down? Two New Studies Point to Phones.  The New York Times
  2. Smartphones broke dating. AI might finish the job.  vox.com
  3. Bombshell Studies Find Surprise Culprit Behind Birthrate Crisis  The Daily Beast
  4. A new kind of birth control: iPhones could be to blame for declining fertility, says study  National Post
  5. Killing The Mood: Smartphones Reduce Birth Rate, Studies Say  Barron's
